title: Palindrome Number
prompt: Given an integer x, return true if it is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards in base 10). Negative numbers are not palindromes.
hints: Negative numbers can never be palindromes due to the leading minus.
Reverse half (or all) of the digits and compare.

optimal.approach: Reverse the integer numerically and compare.

pitfalls: Reversing all digits can overflow in fixed-width languages — reverse only half.
x ending in 0 (and x != 0) cannot be a palindrome.
followups: What if you may not convert to a string?
